# Quay — Autonomous AI Software Factory

Quay is an open-source AI Software Factory for orchestrating autonomous AI agents through customizable pipelines.

## What is Quay?

Quay is a self-hosted AI agents platform that writes code, reviews, security-scans, and deploys — with full cost transparency.

## Key Features

### 🤖 Autonomous Agent Pipelines
- Role-based agents: Engineer → Reviewer → Security → Deployer
- Customizable pipeline stages
- SWE-agent loop: Observe → Plan → Act → Reflect

### 🔌 MCP Integration
- Model Context Protocol support
- Connect any MCP-compatible tool server via stdio
- Lazy-loaded per pipeline stage

### 💰 Cost Transparency
- Every LLM call logged with token counts and cost in USD
- Per-task, per-project, and global cost dashboards
- Route through A3M Router for adaptive multi-provider routing

### 📊 Mission Control Dashboard
- Real-time dark UI with KPI cards
- Kanban board (7 states)
- Live activity feed via SSE streaming

### 🧠 Three-Tier Agent Memory
- Short-term: Per-run cache (30-minute TTL)
- Medium-term: 7-day lessons learned
- Long-term: Agent capability/failure graph

## Installation

```bash
npm install quay-ai-factory
```

## Use Cases

- CI/CD pipelines with autonomous code review
- Security scanning in deployment workflows  
- Self-hosted AI coding agents
- Multi-provider LLM routing with cost tracking

## Comparison

Quay is MIT-licensed, self-hosted, and cost-transparent — unlike enterprise solutions like ServiceNow AI Agents or Copilot Studio.

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/Das-rebel/quay
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/quay-ai-factory
- Docs: https://das-rebel.github.io/quay/

## Technical Stack

- TypeScript/Svelte
- SQLite for persistence
- SSE for real-time updates
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) for tool integration
